items.title: Dragonhunter Low Intensity Power 30k/32.5k/33.5k
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 0 Button Rotation
2:26 0 Button Explanation
2:40 2 Buttons Rotation
5:01 2 Buttons Explanation
5:44 3 Buttons Rotation
8:01 3 Buttons Explanation
10:07 Overall Build Explanation
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the zero-button rundown, and in general with all of these, just auto attack and try not to cancel your auto attack chain. You want to make sure you get that third hit off and get that big symbol off.

For the two-button rundown, your opener is Procession of Blades, which is your trap, into your F1, which is Spear of Justice. You want to time Procession of Blades right before you cast your F1. Basically, just try to time these two skills together. If you do not time them together, the DPS loss is not significant. I think you lose somewhere between 500 and maybe 1,000 DPS. It does not really matter. If you decide to press Procession of Blades off cooldown whenever it is up, you are just losing out on a tiny bit of damage, but if you want to min-max, time Procession of Blades with your F1.

For the three-button rundown, it is the same thing as the two-button. You want to try to time Procession of Blades with your F1. Your opener is Procession of Blades into your F1, and cast Test of Faith whenever it is off cooldown. Again, you want to try to time Procession of Blades with your F1, but if you press Procession of Blades before F1 is up, the DPS loss is not significant. You will probably lose around 500 to 1,000 DPS. If you decide to turn your brain off and cast Procession of Blades whenever it is up, that is fine too. It is not a big deal.

Test of Faith is a small DPS increase for one button, and you do not change the way you play at all. You just press it whenever it is off cooldown. Triggering this gives you Aegis, and it also triggers Relic of the Dragonhunter. Basically, you get 10% more damage for 5 seconds whenever you trigger this trap. If you really want to min-max, you can keep an eye on the Dragonhunter relic icon above the enemy. For example, if I put down Test of Faith and it does not proc, then put down Procession of Blades, you will see a little sword icon above the golem. That means the Dragonhunter relic is active. If you really want to min-max, you could time Test of Faith by watching that icon and cast Test of Faith as a filler when it is no longer up. You will gain a DPS increase, but it probably will not be that significant.

For the build rundown, I will go through all the skills. On hammer, Hammer 2 is a leap. It is really nice to get around. Hammer 3 is an immob, but not really that useful. Hammer 4 is a CC, so make sure to use Hammer 4 if you see a break bar. Hammer 5 technically is also CC, but the opponent basically has to pass through it, so it is maybe situationally useful. Hammer 4 is your big CC button.

This build runs Radiance. If you look through Radiance, you will see the trait Righteous Instincts. Resolution increases your chance to critically strike. Basically, having the Resolution boon on you increases your crit chance by 25%. This is how you crit cap. If you do not have Resolution, you will experience a pretty major DPS loss.

You may be asking how you gain Resolution. You can press your heal skill off cooldown. If you are playing in a group with low Resolution uptime, your healer does not give it to you, or your comp is bad and does not give you Resolution, you can press your heal skill off cooldown. You can replace Test of Faith with, for example, Stand Your Ground! to grant yourself Resolution. Hallowed Ground is also another skill that grants yourself Resolution. Keep an eye on your Resolution uptime and make sure that you have enough Resolution uptime and are critting. If you are not, then try to substitute a little bit.

I will probably leave a link in the description, but there was a variant of this build where you would play Virtues. Virtues is a trait line that you replace Radiance with, and you basically take all of the top traits in Virtues. After testing it out, it seems like they have hammered Virtues quite a bit, and there is very little to no DPS difference between the two, so by default I go with Radiance.

The reason I do not go with Virtues by default, though you can still play Virtues, is that it is going to require a whole bunch of assassin's gear. Check the link in the description. The reason I do not recommend it is because of Unscathed Contender, where you gain a damage increase with Aegis and a damage increase while being above a certain health threshold. I assume that anyone playing this build is probably going to be a little newer, maybe learning the game, and maybe taking a lot of damage and a lot of hits. You are probably not going to have high Aegis uptime, and you may be taking hits and losing health. That is why it is not the default build.

The nice thing about Virtues, even though the damage is now very similar to Radiance with 100% Resolution uptime, is that Virtues does not rely on Resolution. If you decide to play this build and you do not like dealing with Resolution, then check out the Virtues build in the description. It is good. You will probably crit cap, and you do not have to rely on your teammates, Resolution, or looking at any of that. It is still viable, and you still play it the exact same way. The only difference if you play Virtues is that your F1 is going to come off cooldown much quicker because virtues gain reduced recharge. You will be able to use your Procession of Blades and F1 combo a little bit faster. Again, it is viable and very similar in terms of damage, but it is not ahead in damage anymore. It is just an option that you can play.

For everything else, nothing is really that important. It is just damage, damage, damage. You pretty much do not want to touch any of these at all.

For your utilities, I would probably recommend you keep Litany of Wrath. The way this works is that 33% of the damage you deal is returned to you as healing. You can effectively look at this as 6 seconds of invulnerability with how much damage you do. You can face-tank hits and heal yourself really well with Litany of Wrath. I would recommend keeping this skill and using it. If you are playing Radiance, make sure that you spam Litany of Wrath. It has a really short cooldown. Guardian has a lot of really nice utilities.

I should also mention the F skills. Whenever you use F1 with this build, you want to leave it inside the target. You have the ability to recast it, and there is going to be this Justice effect on the target ticking. Do not recast this, because otherwise you will lose your damage from Big Game Hunter. Just leave it in there. If you read it, it says that striking an enemy tethered by your Spear of Justice increases strike damage dealt by 25%, so leave it in there.

F2 is mobility, so you can get around a little quicker and faster. F3 gives Aegis, so you can block attacks from the front. This is useful if you are taking a lot of hits or damage. You can pop this and continue auto attacking with hammer instead of dodging.

For utilities, you can run Dragon's Maw. Dragon's Maw is a CC, so feel free to run Dragon's Maw instead of Signet of Courage. If you decide not to press your elite at all, then run Signet of Courage to get a little bit of extra healing, but otherwise take Dragon's Maw. You can always take Feel My Wrath! if you really want, but this is mostly for superspeed, kind of a niche use if you need to get around. The Quickness and Fury are not going to be as useful because you should have a healer, but it is nice to get around if you need that superspeed.

Bane Signet is CC. You probably do not want to swap this unless you absolutely need to. I would not swap Bane Signet. Procession of Blades I would not swap. With the two-button and three-button builds, you run Relic of the Dragonhunter, which works with your traps, so you want to have at least one trap. If you decide to swap something, swap Test of Faith.

Guardian has a bunch of really nice utility. Judge's Intervention lets you blink to your target and burn them. You select a target, and you blink to it. Merciful Intervention lets you shadowstep to an ally, effectively blinking to an ally. You can pretty much ignore the healing part, or you can use it, but it can be nice to get around if you are out of position and want to get to your allies quickly.

Wall of Reflection is useful because there are a ton of mechanics with projectiles that you can block or reflect. Sometimes you actually do not want to reflect, so watch out for that and listen to your commander if you decide to reflect and are not supposed to.

Hallowed Ground was already mentioned for Resolution uptime. It is also a pretty nice stability skill, so it is kind of two in one. You can consider running it if you want. Sanctuary is a big, good CC skill. The way it works is that you put down Sanctuary, and each pulse does a little bit of CC. Usually Sanctuary will push enemies away from wherever you put it down, but each pulse can do some CC, so it can be a nice CC skill to run. Stand Your Ground! gives extra stability. You could also run Hammer of Wisdom for CC, but you probably do not need it.

I think that is it for this build. I hope you all see success with it and enjoy.

items.title: 3 Button Firebrand Condi 31.9k
items.rotation: Basically spam mantra of flame off cooldown. Fit in axe 2 and torch 4 after an axe auto attack. To make it simplest you can align axe 2 torch 4 casts to be synced like I did.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:24 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
So, to explain the Firebrand builds, you basically just want to cast either skill 2, if you're running the three-button build, which is Symbol of Vengeance, or Purging Flames after an auto attack. You can time your Zealot's Flame either right after you cast Symbol of Vengeance or right after you cast Purging Flames. Your Mantra of Flame you can just dump off cooldown. If you're running the four-button build, Purging Flames is your highest priority. If not, then all you really do with the three-button build is spam Symbol of Vengeance.

The thing about these Firebrand builds is that since they don't use tomes, all of your tomes are free to be used as you wish. I would recommend taking some time to read through what your tome skills do. Some notable ones: skill 3 in Tome of Justice is a pull. Skills 4 and 5 are big AoE damage. This applies Ashes of the Just to all of your allies, so it basically just increases your damage. Tome of Resolve has some notable ones too: skill 4 gives some healing, and skill 5 converts conditions to boons, which is a really nice condition cleanse. If your team is getting nuked with conditions, you can just go into Tome of Resolve, pop 5, and get out. Tome of Resolve skill 2 also removes some conditions.

Then the big one is Tome of Courage. This is why I think this Firebrand build is pretty good. Skill 3 is a reflect, which is really strong on a lot of encounters. Make sure that you know this exists. Skill 5 is just big protection. For you as a DPS, the most notable skill here is the reflect dome in Tome of Courage.

Some useful skills: Litany of Wrath will heal you. This does apply to both strike and condition damage, so it's a useful healing skill for you to run. Mantra of Solace for the most part isn't super useful for you as a DPS, so you can most likely just ignore it. For your elite, Mantra of Liberation is worth considering if you're willing to press it, since it's a stun break and stability for you and your team.

Some other notable Guardian skills are Judge's Intervention, which is a blink to an enemy; Merciful Intervention, which is a blink to an ally; Wall of Reflection, another reflection option, although you'd probably prefer the Tome of Courage one; Hallowed Ground and Stand Your Ground!, which are both good stability options, though you should probably prefer your elite Mantra of Liberation; Sanctuary, which is a really strong CC option; and Hammer of Wisdom if you want some extra CC. Guardian has a lot of good utility.

That's it. Enjoy the build.

items.title: 4 Button Firebrand Condi ~32.5k

This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:22 Rundown

items.title: 4 Button Firebrand Quickness Healer
items.rotation: Use heal, Mantra of Potence, Advance, Feel my Wrath off cooldown.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Also I forgot to mention but if you run staff you can swap out Advance to something else. Or if you decide to use your tome skills you can also swap out Advance if you are granting swiftness. But you do want to make sure you are granting swiftness so use advance if you don't use staff or tomes.

0:00 Rotation
1:15 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, the build is pretty simple. All you really want to do is spam your heal, Mantra of Potence, Advance!, and Feel My Wrath! off cooldown and auto-attack with hammer. It's hard for me to make these low-intensity healer builds because I know that they won't work everywhere, but I try to focus on giving you as much passive healing as I can. You don't need to press your buttons for the most part, but there will be fights where you need burst healing.

If you can, please run staff. Staff is really, really good. Staff 3, Staff 4, and Staff 2 are all really good skills. Unfortunately, staff doesn't have as much passive healing as hammer does, so I still presented hammer, but I would seriously recommend running staff.

Basically, the way the build works is you have your Virtues line, which gives your Virtue skill 2 passive healing and also grants it to nearby allies. Honor is mostly focused on improving healing, giving a little bit of might, and also the trait Writ of Persistence. This is how the build heals for the most part: you heal through your symbols on the ground through hammer. That's where a good majority of your healing is coming from. You also grant might whenever you use up all of your Mantra skills, because the trait Weighty Terms applies slow, which in turn applies Stoic Demeanor. This build runs a few interesting skills. Advance! is usually something you give through Staff 3, but again, staff just doesn't have as much passive healing in this setup. Your only source of swiftness is Advance!. Your elite is a small source of quickness, but the main thing your elite is useful for is fury, because this build doesn't have any fury generation apart from your elite and your relic, the Relic of Altruism. You will grant fury and eight stacks of might whenever you use a healing skill. So your elite and the Relic of Altruism are how you keep up permanent fury uptime, which is really important. I would consider running staff, and I would also consider looking through and using your Tome skills. In these Firebrand builds I don't use them even though maybe I should. When I started making these, I viewed tomes as weapon swapping, and these builds don't have weapon swap. Again, all of your tomes are free to use. Some useful ones: Tome of Justice skill 3 is a pull. Tome of Resolve is your main healing tome. If you're taking a lot of damage and don't want to run staff, skill 5 and skill 4 are big healing skills, and skill 2 is a condi cleanse. Tome of Courage can grant stability, protection, Aegis, a reflect, and pulsing resistance. I would highly encourage you to either run staff and/or use your tome skills, but if you decide to do neither, I think hammer is your best option.

For utility skills, you only really have one flexible utility slot. Hammer 2 is a little mobility. Hammer 3 will proc your Stoic Demeanor, so if you want a little bit of resistance and might, you can use Hammer 3. Hammer 4 is CC. I put Sanctuary by default on this build. Sanctuary is a really strong CC skill, so I would probably just run it. Other strong options are Mantra of Lore for condi removal, Judge's Intervention to blink to an enemy, Merciful Intervention to blink to an ally and res them, Wall of Reflection, Hallowed Ground, Stand Your Ground!, Bane Signet if you need a little more CC, and Bow of Truth if you need more healing.

With these healer builds, it's hard because as a healer you need to be somewhat dynamic to what's happening in the fight, and there are going to be parts where people take a lot of damage and you need to pump out burst healing quickly. I'm trying to focus more on strong passive healing with these builds if you're going to be playing a low-intensity healer. I hope you enjoy the build. Thank you.
